aids.title: AVTI - Consensus Properties
aids.content:
**A**greement - All agree
**V**alidity - Valid proposals only
**T**ermination - Time to decide
**I**ntegrity - Input values only

Remember: 'A Very Timely Integration'

aids.title: CAP Theorem
aids.content:
**C**onsistency - Same data everywhere
**A**vailability - Always responsive
**P**artition tolerance - Works despite splits

Remember: You can only pick 2 of 3!

aids.title: The Restaurant Analogy
aids.content: Imagine 10 friends deciding on a restaurant. Each suggests a place, they share opinions (broadcast), and vote. Even if 2 friends are unreliable (Byzantine), the 8 reliable friends can still reach agreement through majority voting. That's consensus!

aids.title: Crash vs Byzantine Faults
aids.content:
**Crash Fault**: Computer dies - like a friend who stops responding
**Byzantine Fault**: Computer lies - like a friend who deliberately gives wrong directions

Byzantine faults are HARDER to handle!

aids.title: BFT Node Formula
aids.content:
For f Byzantine nodes:

n >= 3f + 1 (total nodes needed)

Example: To tolerate 2 bad nodes, need 7 total
3(2) + 1 = 7

aids.title: Consensus Rhyme
aids.content:
Nodes scattered far and wide,
Must decide, can't hide.
Agree on truth, no central guide,
Consensus keeps the network tied!
